danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite animation showing aggressive stance or punch wind-up
danger_signals.meaning: Risk of being punched and losing ball control or position

danger_signals.visual_form: Soda cans and candy wrappers rendered on court surface
danger_signals.meaning: Tripping hazard that causes ball handler to fall and lose possession

danger_signals.visual_form: Referee sprite in collision path on court
danger_signals.meaning: Hazard that can cause player to stumble similar to floor objects

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent character in ball-holding animation closer to basket
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate scoring threat requiring defensive action

safety_signals.meaning: Control of play and immediate scoring opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Spatial separation between player sprites on court
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced immediate threat of being punched or stealing attempts

safety_signals.visual_form: Unobstructed visual line from player position to goal
safety_signals.meaning: Safe opportunity to move toward scoring position

faction_indicators.color_identity: Team-specific color scheme (customizable per arcade configuration)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Team name displayed on scoreboard and player uniforms
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scoreboard position and sideline positioning indicates home team

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Different team name on scoreboard and uniforms
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Opposite scoreboard position indicates away team status

faction_indicators.color_identity: Chicago, Los Angeles, New York, Denver, Natural High, Brawl State each with distinct color palettes
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Team names serve as primary identifier
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Team colors visible on uniforms and scoreboard display

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scoreboard shows tied or low scores; center court positioning with both teams relatively fresh
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players move at standard speed; both teams actively competing for ball

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scoreboard displays point differential visually emphasized; leading team positioned favorably
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Trailing team may display more aggressive punch attempts and stealing behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scoreboard emphasizes tie condition; sudden death visual indicator (if present in UI)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Both teams exhibit maximum aggression; every possession treated as final scoring opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shot clock display becomes critical visual; scoreboard position emphasizes remaining time
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased frequency of punch attempts and aggressive stealing mechanics; faster gameplay pace

environmental_storytelling.example: Soda cans and candy wrappers scattered on court floor create environmental obstacles that communicate a casual, street-basketball atmosphere rather than pristine professional arena
Arcade operator ability to rename teams to local heroes or real teams establishes local competitive context and community ownership
Eight playable characters with unique characteristics (not detailed in source but mentioned as present) visually differentiate player archetypes and playstyles

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Scoreboard continuously displays quarter, score, and time remaining
environmental_storytelling.meaning: Primary visual communication of game pressure, team standing, and remaining opportunity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Falling animation triggered by stepping on floor objects, dropping ball for opponent pickup
Punch animation toward opponent; flying leap tackle that pulls down opponent shorts (arcade version only)
Movement toward ball location; call-out animation for teammate pass
Stumble/fall animation similar to hazard collision, allowing opponent ball steal
Basket completion animation; scoreboard updates immediately

notes: Arch Rivals communicates world-state primarily through scoreboard information and court positioning rather than atmospheric visual elements. Danger is signaled mechanically through sprite proximity and floor hazard placement rather than color-coded warnings. Faction identity is lightweight—distinguished primarily by uniform colors and team names rather than narrative or symbolic depth. The game emphasizes street-level, casual basketball competition through environmental details (litter on court) and aggressive mechanics (punching, short-pulling) that contrast with professional sports presentation. NES version has reduced visual fidelity compared to arcade, limiting some manifestation clarity.
